title: Tracer
subtitle: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod te
body:
Tracer is a data aggregation and reporting tool used to help marketers analyze their ad campaigns and media spending strategies across platforms. It specializes in creating highly customizable reports and gives clients greater clarity on where they should be investing and why. In addition to reporting, Tracer also offers a variety of additional features including ad verification, offline data conversions, custom labeling, and analytics integration.

To date, Tracer has tracked over one billion in spending across 50 brands globally. Their client base includes Mondelēz International, Chase, and Sanofi, most of whom receive consultation services for additional analysis and support. The team continues to improve the tool, remaining a front runner in the adtech landscape.

sections.text: Tracer uses three strategies to set it apart from the adtech industry. First, it puts the client’s data sources all in one place. It interfaces with most top ad buying platforms (20 different platforms in total) including Google Ads, Facebook, LinkedIn, Pinterest, and Snapchat. This allows for greater transparency of cross-platform media performance and eliminates the tedious task of aggregating data manually. Second, it allows the user to create automated rules and configurations to define how this data is combined and organized. This gives greater control over the resulting reports and draws attention to numbers that matter most. Lastly, the resulting reports are made in a way that is actionable and impactful, as well as flexible to use across a variety of visualization tools such as Chartio, Looker, and Tableau.

title: University of Michigan Alumni Association Website
body: The University of Michigan Alumni Association has a chapter based in New York City to bring together alumni for various events and activities. I serve as the webmaster for the chapter, managing the website and making updates when needed.. My current focus is to redesign each page for consistency, usability, and mobile friendliness.
skills: HTML, CSS, Javascript, Higher Logic Content Manager, Adobe InDesign
client: University of Michigan Alumni Association, New York Chapter

title: Cigna Digital Experience Standards
body:
• A project for marketing to supply standards for all digital teams in order to make Cigna's online presence more consistent
• Contributed to the design and development of the project by working between the marketing department and Cigna Content Management Services

title: Cigna Hong Kong Website
body: In 2016, Cigna Hong Kong agreed to move their website onto Cigna’s  global content management platform - HP Teamsite. For this effort, GCMS was tasked with revamping the site, and building it into HP Teamsite so the Hong Kong team could continue to manage the site with minimal technical skill. The project required significant collaboration between UI/UX, developers, content translators, QA, project managers, and business managers across both countries. My role as a developer was to translate mock ups into teamsite components, and build the pages using these components. The site was completed in less than a month in both English and Chinese.

title: Cigna Individual & Family Plans
body:
• Led the development effort of the annual release of Cigna Individual and Family Plans for Open Enrollment
• Moved the plan finder application from Java to AngularJS
• Automated data processing to format the plan details for the plan finder application
skills: HP Teamsite, Python, HTML, CSS, Javascript

title: Cigna Medicare Marketing Site
body:
• Participated in the annual release of Cigna HealthSpring-Medicare website
• Used python to automate data processing from various spreadsheets to one JSON file used by the plan finder application
• Content updates and defect corrections leading up to the release
skills: HP Teamsite, Python, HTML, CSS, Javascript
